3.happening: Loading the dictionary words into a set for instant lookup.
3.why: Membership tests must be O(1) inside the double loop.
3.invariant: words holds every allowed dictionary word.

4.happening: Measuring the length of the string.
4.why: The dp table needs one cell per cut position from 0 to n.
4.invariant: n is fixed for the whole run.

5.happening: Creating the reachability table with every prefix not-yet-reachable (0).
5.why: dp[i] will record whether the first i characters split into words.
5.invariant: dp[i] means the first i characters are segmentable.

6.happening: Base case: dp[0] = 1 — the empty prefix is trivially reachable.
6.why: Segmenting zero characters needs no words.

7.happening: Deciding whether prefix length {i} is reachable.
7.why: A prefix is reachable if some earlier cut plus a word lands exactly here.
7.invariant: All shorter prefixes are already decided.

8.happening: Scanning earlier cut {j} as the possible start of the last word.
8.why: The last word could begin at any earlier cut.
8.invariant: j ranges over every cut before i.

9.happening: Slicing out the candidate last word from cut {j} to cut {i}.
9.why: This piece is the word that would finish the prefix.
9.invariant: piece is the substring between the two cuts.

10.happening: Looking up whether the piece '{piece}' is in the dictionary.
10.why: Only a real dictionary word can be the last word of a segmentation.
10.invariant: fits is true only for dictionary words.

11.happening: Prefix at cut {j} is reachable and '{piece}' is a word — prefix {i} is reachable!
11.why: A reachable prefix followed by a dictionary word extends reachability.
11.invariant: dp[i] is about to become reachable.

11.happening: Cut {j} reachable? dp[{j}] = {dp[j]}; is '{piece}' a word? {fits} — no extension from here.
11.why: Both a reachable prefix and a real word are required to extend.

12.why: We found a reachable cut plus a word ending exactly at i.
12.invariant: dp[i] is now reachable and stays reachable.

13.happening: Reading the final answer: is the full length {n} reachable? dp[{n}] = {dp[n]}.
13.why: The whole string is segmentable exactly when dp[n] is reachable.

14.happening: Returning {result}: whether the string splits into dictionary words.
14.why: The table is complete, so dp[n] is final.

init.happening: Setting up the word set and the reachability table.
init.why: The DP needs fast word lookup and a base case.
init.invariant: dp[i] means the first i characters are segmentable.

loop.happening: Deciding each prefix by scanning earlier cuts.
loop.why: Reachability of a prefix depends on shorter reachable prefixes.

update.happening: Forming the candidate last word and testing it.
update.why: The last word of a segmentation is a substring ending here.
update.invariant: piece and fits describe the candidate word.

check.happening: Testing whether a reachable prefix plus a word reaches here.
check.why: Both a reachable prefix and a real word are required.
check.invariant: dp holds decided reachability so far.

return.happening: Reading out whether the whole string is reachable.
